pattern: Counting lit lamps per row, column, and both diagonal keys lets each query answer illumination in constant time, then turning off the queried neighbourhood updates those counters.
hints: A cell is lit if any lamp shares its row, its column, or either diagonal, so track how many lamps cover each of those lines rather than the grid itself.
Encode the two diagonals by row plus column and by row minus column; a lamp contributes one to its row, column, and both diagonal tallies.

Keep a set of active lamp coordinates and four counters; a query is illuminated when any of its four line tallies is positive, then for the queried cell and its eight neighbours remove still-active lamps and decrement their lines.

edge_inputs.description: second query goes dark after neighbours switch off
querying the same lit cell twice, the lamp itself was turned off
row sharing keeps a later cell lit
single cell grid with one lamp
duplicate lamp listings count once toward illumination
no lamps so every query is dark

stress.description: moderate grid with random lamps and queries that overlap their neighbourhoods
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 50))
    nl = rng.randint(0, size)
    nq = rng.randint(1, size)
    lamps = [[rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)] for _ in range(nl)]
    queries = [[rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)] for _ in range(nq)]
    return [n, lamps, queries]
stress.note: small n forces overlapping rows/cols/diagonals so toggling matters
